summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a malicious npm package tarball with '../' path traversal entries causes the esm.sh server's extractPackageTarball function to write files outside the intended package directory. The proof file /tmp/evohunt-pwned.js is created containing the attacker-controlled proof marker.
expected_security_impact: Requesting a malicious npm package from the esm.sh server should cause the extractPackageTarball function to write a file at /tmp/evohunt-pwned.js (outside the intended npm package directory) containing the proof marker string EVOHUNT_POC_PATH_TRAVERSAL_SUCCESS.
actual_observed_result: The esm.sh server downloaded the malicious tarball from the mock npm registry, extracted it, and the path traversal entry 'package/../../../../../../../../../../../../../../../../../../tmp/evohunt-pwned.js' was resolved by path.Join to '/tmp/evohunt-pwned.js'. The file was created with the proof marker content, confirming arbitrary file write.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks for the existence of /tmp/evohunt-pwned.js and verifies it contains the exact proof marker string 'EVOHUNT_POC_PATH_TRAVERSAL_SUCCESS'. This distinguishes a genuine path traversal write from normal package extraction (which only writes inside the package directory) or a server startup failure.
evidence_produced_by_run: The file /tmp/evohunt-pwned.js exists outside the intended package directory at /esm/npm/evil-pkg@1.0.0/node_modules/evil-pkg/
The file contains the proof marker string 'EVOHUNT_POC_PATH_TRAVERSAL_SUCCESS' confirming it was written by the malicious tarball extraction
The malicious tarball entry used 20 levels of '../' to traverse from the package directory to the filesystem root

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository (esm-dev/esm.sh) on the host, checks out the v136_1 tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
